channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Croatia's President Milanović overwhelming favorite to win reelection in a runoff vote
item.description: Croatia’s President Zoran Milanović is the overwhelming favorite to win reelection against a ruling conservative party candidate in a runoff presidential vote.
item.dc:creator: Sasa Kavic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia says it will continue oil and gas projects despite US sanctions
item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has denounced U.S. sanctions against Moscow's energy sector and said the country would carry on with large oil and gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trams collide in the French city of Strasbourg, injuring dozens, though none critically
item.description: Firefighters say two trams have collided in Strasbourg in eastern France, injuring dozens of people, though none critically.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Syrian intelligence agency says it thwarted a planned Islamic State attack on a Shiite shrine
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Intelligence officials in Syria's new de facto government thwarted a plan by the Islamic State group to set off a bomb at a Shiite shrine in the Damascus suburb of Sayyida Zeinab, state media reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greenland PM 'ready' to talk with Trump
item.description: Greenland Prime Minister Múte Egede said he is “ready” to talk with President-elect Trump, who has recently expressed ambitions for the U.S. to acquire Greenland, the world’s largest island.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: One of four lynx captured in the Scottish Highlands dies
item.description: LONDON (AP) — One of four lynx thought to have been released illegally in the Scottish Highlands has died within hours after it was captured, wildlife authorities said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine says it captured 2 North Korean soldiers fighting for Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s forces have captured two North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Saturday, the first such claim by Kyiv since North Korea sent thousands of troops to shore up Moscow's war effort on the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkey's Kurdish leaders meet jailed politician as the two sides inch toward peace
item.description: A delegation from one of Turkey’s biggest pro-Kurdish political parties has met a leading figure of the Kurdish movement in prison, the latest step in a tentative process to end the country’s 40-year conflict.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Wilks, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Uyghurs detained in Thailand say they face deportation and persecution in China
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A group of Uyghur men who were detained in Thailand over a decade ago say that the Thai government is preparing to deport them to China, alarming activists and family members who say the men are at risk of abuse and torture if they are sent back.
item.dc:creator: DAKE KANG and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s military retakes a strategic city from RSF rebels, dealing them a major blow
item.description: Sudanese officials says the military and its allies had taken back the strategic city of Wad Medani more than a year after the Rapid Support Forces seized it.
item.dc:creator: By Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As Jean-Marie Le Pen is buried, here's a look at key dates of the rise of the far right in France
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s main far-right party and a polarizing figure in French politics, is being buried Saturday in a private family ceremony in his hometown of La Trinité-sur-Mer in Brittany.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro takes new oath amid protests and international rebuke
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a new term on Friday, extending his increasingly repressive rule in the face of renewed protests and rebukes from the United States and others who believe he stole last year's vote.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salvage complete of tanker at center of oil spill scare after being hit by Houthi rebels in Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea after being attacked by Yemen's Houthi rebels and threatening a massive oil spill has been salvaged, a security firm said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: ABOARD THE EML SAKALA IN THE BALTIC SEA (AP) — As they plied the gray, icy waters of the Baltic Sea west of Russia on Thursday, the crew of the Estonian minehunter EML Sakala kept a careful eye on any vessels slowing down suspiciously or suddenly changing course.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and HENDRIK OSULA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party to decide on March 9 who will succeed Trudeau as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's governing Liberal Party will announce the country's next prime minister on March 9 after a leadership vote that follows the resignation of Justin Trudeau this week, party leaders said late Thursday. Trudeau remains prime minister until a new leader is chosen.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia open to Trump, Putin meeting: Kremlin
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is ready to meet President-elect Trump without conditions, the Kremlin said Friday, but is waiting for more specifics following the U.S. presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: South Korea’s acting leader accepts resignation of presidential security chief
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader on Friday accepted the resignation of the chief of the presidential security service, Park Jong-joon, as he faced police questioning over how his forces blocked law enforcement efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moldovans grapple with outages as energy crisis grips pro-Russia region
item.description: COPANCA, Moldova (AP) — Every time planned electrical outages are imposed on his village in southern Moldova, 73-year-old retiree Vasili Donici passes the time solving crosswords and puzzles in a room he illuminates with a small gas lamp.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and AUREL OBREJA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Emhoff cancel trip to Singapore, Middle East, Germany amid California fires
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff announced their decision to cancel their upcoming international trips on Thursday in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau: Trump talk of annexing Canada a distraction, won't happen
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday he thinks President-elect Trump’s public musings about annexing the neighbor to the north is a distraction from Trump’s threat to impose steep tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
